title: Choose Python TUI stack and app architecture
description: Why this exists: the client should be maintainable and testable, with transport, protocol, state, and presentation separated. What needs to be done: choose the TUI framework and libraries, likely Textual/Rich or a similarly testable terminal UI stack; define module boundaries for transports, protocol adapters, app state, view models, widgets, command handling, and persistence. Acceptance criteria: library choices are documented in Beads notes or implementation docs; transport interfaces allow BLE and IP/CoAP implementations behind one app model; unit-testable boundaries are defined; dependency footprint is acceptable for macOS and Linux developer machines.
notes:
Python TUI stack/architecture decision draft. Library choices: keep Textual as the TUI framework because python/pyproject.toml already depends on textual, exposes lichen-tui, and lichen/src/lichen/tui/app.py is already Textual-based. Use Textual widgets/App/Pilot for terminal UI and snapshots; use Rich only through Textual/Rich renderables, not as a separate app framework. Use aiocoap + cbor2 for IP/CoAP LCI resource access. Use existing lichen.slip codec and pyserial for serial/USB SLIP. BLE direct LCI transport should use bleak, but only as an optional extra; current pyproject has bleak under the meshtastic extra, so packaging follow-up should decide whether to create a native-client or ble extra before implementing BLE. Keep any Meshtastic/MeshCore compatibility dependencies out of the native TUI core. Dependency footprint: Textual, aiocoap, cbor2, anyio, pyserial are already in the base dependency set; bleak is optional and works on macOS/Linux but has OS-specific BLE behavior, so BLE smoke tests remain separate from no-hardware unit tests. Version policy: local lock currently resolves Textual 8.2.7 while pyproject lower bound is textual>=0.80.0; implementation should either code to a conservative Textual API subset or raise/pin a tested lower bound in project-LICHEN-py4c.1.11 packaging. Architecture modules under python/src/lichen/tui should be split before feature work: transports.py for Transport ABC plus IpCoapTransport, SerialSlipTransport, BleLciTransport, DemoTransport; lci_client.py for CoAP discovery/resource operations independent of UI; resources.py or models.py for typed resource payload dataclasses/TypedDicts (Status, Identity, RadioConfig, Neighbor, Route, Message, Capability); store.py for immutable-ish AppState, ResourceState loading/empty/error values, conversation drafts, selected target, and stale timestamps; view_models.py for converting AppState to table rows/status labels with all truncation and 80x24 priority rules from project-LICHEN-py4c.1.2; screens/ for DashboardScreen, ChatsScreen, ThreadScreen, NodesScreen, NodeDetailScreen, MeshScreen, ConfigScreen, LogsScreen, DiagnosticsScreen, HelpOverlay; widgets/ for StatusBar, CommandBar, BoundedTable, ComposeBar, StatePanel, KeyHelp; commands.py for user intents/actions and command routing; persistence.py for recent endpoints, UI preferences, and local message/draft cache using platformdirs or XDG-style path after packaging decision; app.py as thin composition/wiring. Transport interface: connect/disconnect, connection_state async stream, request(method,path,payload,observe=False), observe(path), discover(), close(); BLE/serial/IP all yield the same CoAP resource model. For BLE LCI specifically, transport is BLE GATT SLIP-framed IPv6 per docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md and spec/11-lci.md, not the stale spec/lichen-native CBOR draft. Protocol adapter: LciClient owns /.well-known/core discovery, GET/PUT /config, GET /config/radio, GET /config/identity, GET/Observe /status and /status/neighbors, GET /status/routes, and capability-gated optional resources. It must expose capabilities by discovered resource and response codes, not by hard-coded assumptions. Ambiguous resources are gated by follow-ups: raw diagnostics disabled until project-LICHEN-py4c.1.13; messaging discovery/fallback follows project-LICHEN-py4c.1.14; mesh proxy direct-vs-/mesh-vs-/proxy follows project-LICHEN-py4c.1.15. Unit-test boundaries: transport fakes should test LciClient without Textual; view model tests should validate row priority, truncation, state text, unsupported-feature labels, and delivery/config semantics without network; Textual Pilot/snapshot tests should cover screen navigation and 80x24/60-column layouts with fake Store; command tests should verify key/intents map to state changes; persistence tests should use temp dirs. Implementation order recommended by dependencies: first shared models/store/lci_client with fake transport (py4c.1.4), then Textual shell/widgets (py4c.1.7), then messaging/status/config flows. Acceptance status pending explorer/review: decision recorded in Beads, supports BLE and IP/CoAP behind one app model, unit-testable boundaries defined, dependency footprint acceptable with bleak optional.
Explorer/refinement addendum: Use a shared python/src/lichen/client package as the non-UI app model, with Textual confined to lichen.tui and browser rendering confined to lichen.dashboard. Recommended names: lichen.client.transport for PacketTransport and transport adapters; lichen.client.lci or protocol for LciSession and resource adapters; lichen.client.model for dataclasses/TypedDicts; lichen.client.store for reducer/event state; lichen.client.viewmodel for pure screen models; lichen.client.commands for typed intents; lichen.client.persistence for endpoint/draft/cache storage. lichen.dashboard should eventually consume the same client/protocol layer instead of its private _fetch helper, but dashboard refactor is not required to close this architecture Bead. Keep lichen.coap.resources as server/resource definitions and do not import server resource classes into the client UI. Transport interface refinement: define both PacketTransport for BLE/serial SLIP IPv6 packet links and LciSession for discovered CoAP resource operations; direct IP/CoAP can skip PacketTransport internally but still implements LciSession. Existing lichen.tui.app is simulator-focused around SimRadio and should be treated as reusable Textual example code, not the production native-client architecture. Dependency risks recorded in follow-up project-LICHEN-py4c.1.16: broad Textual/Starlette/aiocoap lower bounds, private aiocoap transport hook risk, and unpinned HTMX CDN. Existing follow-ups already cover messaging path split project-LICHEN-py4c.1.14 and mesh proxy ambiguity project-LICHEN-py4c.1.15; stale spec/lichen-native/raw diagnostics are covered by project-LICHEN-py4c.1.13. Closure criteria remain satisfied because the stack and architecture are chosen while unresolved protocol/dependency details are explicitly tracked.
close_reason: Completed Python TUI stack and architecture decision: Textual/Rich UI shell, shared lichen.client app model, PacketTransport/LciSession separation for BLE/serial/IP CoAP, typed state/view-model/widget boundaries, test seams, and dependency/protocol follow-ups recorded; two reviews found no blocking findings.
